question: I need to charge my electric car but I'd rather avoid congested streets. Where should I head?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find available charging stations AND avoid congested streets (implies finding zones with high/critical congestion to exclude) | MAP: smart-charging-stations-mock / GET /charging-station (available=true) + smart-traffic-monitoring-mock / GET /traffic-sensor (congestionLevel=high OR critical) | CHAIN: none (mock constraint: one filter per request, cannot combine zoneId with congestionLevel in one call; must fetch all congested sensors and filter client-side or via SQL) | FILTER: charging-station?available=true; traffic-sensor?congestionLevel=high (then critical) | VALIDATE: ✓ two GETs needed, one for stations, one for congestion; will use SQL to join and exclude congested zones

input.sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM get_available_charging_stations c WHERE c.zoneId NOT IN (SELECT zoneId FROM get_high_congestion_sensors UNION SELECT zoneId FROM get_critical_congestion_sensors)

question: I want to spend a nice afternoon out. Where in the city has clean air, something interesting to see, and parking available?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: clean air sensors, open attractions, available parking | MAP: smart-environment-and-temperature-sensors-mock / GET /sensor, smart-tourist-attractions-mock / GET /attraction, smart-parking-mock / GET /parking-spot | CHAIN: SQL join on zoneId | FILTER: sensorType=air_quality AND alertActive=false; status=open; available=true | VALIDATE: ✓

input.sql_query: SELECT DISTINCT s.zoneId FROM get_clean_air_zones s JOIN get_open_attractions a ON s.zoneId = a.zoneId JOIN get_available_parking p ON s.zoneId = p.zoneId WHERE s.alertActive = false

question: Which parts of the city are in the worst shape right now, with street lights off, full bins, and traffic problems all at once?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find zones with lights=off, bins=full, traffic=incidentDetected | MAP: smart-lighting-mock GET /light, smart-bins-mock GET /bin, smart-traffic-monitoring-mock GET /traffic-sensor | CHAIN: SQL join on zoneId to find intersection | FILTER: status=off, status=full, incidentDetected=true | VALIDATE: ✓

input.sql_query: SELECT l.zoneId FROM get_off_lights l INNER JOIN get_full_bins b ON l.zoneId = b.zoneId INNER JOIN get_traffic_incidents t ON l.zoneId = t.zoneId

question: I want somewhere calm to go, with something to do and a place to park. What would you suggest?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find open attractions (something to do), find free parking spots (place to park), filter for calm zones (low visitor count) | MAP: smart-tourist-attractions-mock / GET /attraction, smart-parking-mock / GET /parking-spot | CHAIN: SQL join on zoneId to correlate attractions and parking, filter by status and visitor count | FILTER: attraction status=open, parking status=free, calm defined as low currentVisitors | VALIDATE: ✓

input.sql_query: SELECT a.id as attraction_id, a.name as attraction_name, a.zoneId, a.currentVisitors, p.id as parking_id, p.location as parking_location FROM get_open_attractions a JOIN get_free_parking p ON a.zoneId = p.zoneId WHERE a.currentVisitors < 50 ORDER BY a.currentVisitors ASC LIMIT 5
